stories.headline: Asian stocks rise after Trump hints at Iran war deal
stories.summary: Chipmaker-heavy indices gain ahead of blockbuster SpaceX listing

stories.headline: Copper Rallies From Three-Week Low as Trump Flags Iran Deal
stories.summary: Copper rebounded from its lowest close since mid-May after President Donald Trump claimed the US was on the cusp of ending the war with Iran that’s threatening the global economy.

stories.headline: Stock markets surge as Trump calls off strikes on Iran, touts peace deal
stories.summary: Wall Street and Asian markets rally on hopes for an end to the US-Israel war on Iran.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Trump claims Tehran deal ‘approved’, cancels new strikes
stories.summary: The International Rescue Committee warns that people displaced by Israeli attacks in Lebanon are at 'breaking point'.

stories.headline: Donald Trump says US close to deal with Iran and calls off strikes
stories.summary: Markets rally and oil prices dip as US president claims agreement will reopen Strait of Hormuz

stories.headline: Trump Insists Iran Deal Is Close After Scrapping Strikes
stories.summary: President Donald Trump pulled back threatened military strikes against Iran, a reversal that came after he vowed to hit the Islamic Republic “VERY HARD” and threatened to seize its oil infrastructure. Bloomberg's Jill Disis has the latest. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Asia energy crisis at ‘worst’ case scenario, ADB warns
stories.summary: Multilateral lender says 15 countries have requested $4bn in emergency support due to war in Iran

stories.headline: Israel launches overnight strikes across Gaza
stories.summary: Videos shared by Palestinian activists show powerful Israeli airstrikes across Gaza Strip overnight.

stories.headline: White House shows off cage match arena as corruption lawsuit looms
stories.summary: White House officials previewed the grounds where they’ll host a cage match to celebrate US President Trump’s birthday.

stories.headline: Jailed South Korea ex-president gets 30 more years for sending drones into North
stories.summary: Prosecutors argued that Yoon ordered the operation to provoke Pyongyang and create a pretext for martial law.

stories.headline: CLSA name to vanish after four decades in Asian brokerage
stories.summary: Distinctive brand to be replaced by Citic, China’s state-owned financial giant, from next year

stories.headline: Corporate Profits Hit Record High Versus India’s Economy Even as Stocks Struggle
stories.summary: Companies are upbeat about demand and capital spending, ICICI Securities says.

stories.headline: Lloyds Taps Japan’s Booming Samurai Market With ¥75 Billion Sale
stories.summary: Lloyds Banking Group Plc became the latest borrower to sell bonds in the booming Samurai market, where issuance has reached the highest level since fiscal year 2015 amid strong demand from yield-seeking investors.

stories.headline: Jakarta Deploys Thousands of Police as Students Protest Prabowo
stories.summary: Indonesian authorities will deploy thousands of police and soldiers ahead of a planned protest by students, who are angry at the rising cost of living and want President Prabowo Subianto to rein in big-ticket spending and revive the economy.

stories.headline: Chinese Region Pushes Coal Chemicals as Energy Security Fix
stories.summary: One of China’s top coal-producing regions plans to expand use of the feedstock to manufacture chemicals, even as the practice poses a rising threat to the country’s climate goals.

stories.headline: Supreme Court Blocks Alabama From Executing Inmate Using Nitrogen Gas
stories.summary: The unsigned decision for now spares Jeffery Lee, a convicted murderer, and could lead to a broader fight over the relatively new execution method.

stories.headline: Australia news live: Matt Canavan says ‘good on One Nation’ for its fundraiser attacking Labor; national internet outages blamed on fibre provider
stories.summary: Nationals leader says he welcomes ‘everybody trying to take down this government’. stories.headline: Can Africa turn its population boom into prosperity?
stories.summary: Africa’s rapid population growth offers major economic opportunities but also deep structural constraints.

Oil Extends Decline After Trump Says Deal With Iran Is Close
stories.summary: Oil extended declines after President Donald Trump said a peace deal with Iran could be signed as soon as the weekend following US military strikes that cast doubt over progress to end the war.

stories.headline: Deadly Sudan drone strike targets funeral procession
stories.summary: Rights groups accuse the RSF paramilitary group of killing civilians in el-Obeid, a city on the civil war's front line.

stories.headline: Trump says he is cancelling fresh strikes on Iran and hints at possible peace deal
stories.summary: Trump’s social media post comes after the US and Iran traded strikes for a third day, pushing the Middle East closer to the resumption of a full-scale war.

stories.headline: Israel deports French journalist accused of supporting Hamas
stories.summary: Isreal suppressed coverage of its war on Gaza, banning international press and killing hundreds of local journalists.

stories.headline: UK defence funding crisis has been a long time coming
stories.summary: John Healey’s complaint is that Starmer sat on this problem for months before making a derisory offerJohn Healey’s resignation as defence secretary on Thursday was a long time brewing, though in the end the denouement was swift. It leaves an already weak Keir Starmer without a defence strategy less than a month before a Nato summit and an unresolved row about spending as Donald Trump threatens to restart the bombing of Iran.On Monday, No 10 final

stories.headline: Thai court sentences two Uyghur men to death for 2015 Bangkok bombing
stories.summary: Twenty people were killed and 120 injured in the attack at the Erawan Shrine, a popular tourist destinationA Thai court has handed out death sentences to two Uyghur men from the north-western Chinese region of Xinjiang for a 2015 bombing in the centre of Bangkok that killed 20 people.The explosion occurred at the Erawan Shrine in the centre of Bangkok, an area popular with foreign tourists. As well as the 20 people killed, another 120 were injure

stories.headline: Trump to pick US attorney Jay Clayton for director of national intelligence
stories.summary: Nomination comes amid pushback over Donald Trump's selection of Bill Pulte as acting director of the agency.

stories.headline: US slaps sanctions on Cuba's oil and gas company, accusing it of weaponising energy
stories.summary: Cuba is already struggling under a decades-old embargo and a lack of petroleum as the US keeps pushing for a change in its economic and political model.

stories.headline: Russia 'lost standing’ despite ‘a breather’ from higher oil prices, IMF chief says
stories.summary: While Russia has emerged as one of the very few winners from the recent disruptions to the global oil market amid the Iran war, this is not enough to offset the hit of sanctions and isolation, the managing director of the International Monetary Fund (IMF) told Euronews.

stories.headline: IMF warns against further relaxation of euro area fiscal rules
stories.summary: The IMF has urged euro area countries to tighten their fiscal policies rather than relaxing them further in response to the energy crisis, with an extra adjustment effort required from high-debt nations.

stories.headline: World Cup kicks off in Mexico with Shakira, dancing, and protests
stories.summary: There was a star-studded opening ceremony, and some sporadic clashes between police and protesters around the Azteca stadium.

stories.headline: Trump Picks Jay Clayton for Director of National Intelligence After Backlash Over Bill Pulte
stories.summary: The president said he would nominate Jay Clayton, the U.S. attorney in Manhattan and the former chairman of the Securities and Exchange Commission, for the permanent role.

stories.headline: Trump Unveils UFC ‘Claw’ Ahead of Cage Match at the White House
stories.summary: The towering claw will be the site of an Ultimate Fighting Championship cage match on Sunday, which is President Trump’s 80th birthday.

stories.headline: Avril Haines, Former Intelligence Chief, Will Lead Carnegie Endowment for International Peace
stories.summary: In the run-up to the invasion of Ukraine, Ms. Haines helped devise the strategy of declassifying evidence of Russia’s military buildup in an effort to rally European allies.

stories.headline: Vance Boelter Pleads Guilty in Assassination of Minnesota Lawmaker
stories.summary: Federal prosecutors said they would not seek the death penalty for Vance Boelter, who still faces charges in state court.

stories.headline: Justice Dept. Pushes Limits of Its Power Over State Elections
stories.summary: The department once tried to stay out of state elections, urging caution. It is now pressing forward with claims of fraud as President Trump revives his unfounded assertions that elections cannot be trusted.

stories.headline: China bars Philippines defence chief as South China Sea spat sails onwards
stories.summary: Chinese individuals and organisations are also banned from doing business with Gilberto Teodoro and his family.

stories.headline: Meloni tells Brussels to scrap 'rebates' for some member states – or Italy will demand them too
stories.summary: The Italian Prime Minister has attacked the EU's disbursement to so-called frugal states and warned Brussels that Rome's patience with them is at an end.

stories.headline: French singer Patrick Bruel under formal investigation for rape
stories.summary: Bruel was released from custody under judicial supervision while an examining magistrate looks more deeply into the case.

stories.headline: Thousands march in Pakistan-administered Kashmir as clashes kill at least 15
stories.summary: An uneasy silence has blanketed the capital, with empty streets, closed shops and patrolling police.

stories.headline: The AI public market floodgates are opening
stories.summary: The huge sums that Wall Street is about to be asked for look like being only a down payment

stories.headline: How to Trade the SpaceX IPO in Asia's Locked-Out Markets
stories.summary: Investors across Asia have been largely shut out of the world’s largest-ever initial public offering, which has forced them to find creative ways to make bets on SpaceX’s $75 billion global spectacle.

stories.headline: Despite Rout, Indonesia's Danantara Wealth Fund Prices First Dollar Bond for Global Investors
stories.summary: Indonesian sovereign wealth fund Danantara raised $1.5 billion through a successful debut global dollar bond sale, offering relief to President Prabowo Subianto’s government as it tries to stem a selloff in the nation’s assets.

stories.headline: Scaffolding Company’s Data-Center Pivot Cuts Into Margins
stories.summary: Brand Industrial Services Inc., a scaffolding and industrial services company that does business as BrandSafway, reported sharply lower first-quarter earnings as higher costs and spending on its push into data center construction weighed on results, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Blackstone Said to Be in Talks to Buy Canada Property Firm H&R REIT
stories.summary: Blackstone Inc. is in discussions with Canada’s H&R Real Estate Investment Trust about a potential acquisition, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

stories.headline: CME Announces Plans to Offer 24/7 WTI Oil and Gold Contracts
stories.summary: The Chicago Mercantile Group announced plans to offer 24-hour, seven-days-a-week trading in new, smaller crude oil futures and its existing gold contract, the exchange said Thursday.

stories.headline: Ecuador to Cut Beer Prices 20% in World Cup Show of ‘Support’
stories.summary: Ecuador is cutting beer prices by more than 20% through a temporary tax cut to rally “extra support” for the national football team during the World Cup, President Daniel Noboa said.

stories.headline: Twenty-five anti-Isaac Herzog protesters to face joint trial in Sydney

stories.headline: Ryanair investigated over charging parents to sit with their children
stories.summary: Budget airline describes inquiry as ‘bogus’ as watchdog says it is only large carrier flying from UK to impose chargeBusiness live – latest updatesEurope’s biggest low-cost airline, Ryanair, is facing an investigation over the mandatory fee it charges a parent to sit with their child.The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) said the Irish carrier’s terms and conditions require at least one parent to sit with their children, including those wit

stories.headline: Minister condemns ‘racist thuggery’ as violence returns to Northern Ireland
stories.summary: The return of unrest to the province, which suffered decades of sectarian violence, has provoked deep concern.

stories.headline: Cyprus proposes €32.8bn cut to next EU budget as compromise between rival camps
stories.summary: Cyprus proposes a 2% cut — worth €32.8bn — to the EU's draft €2 trillion budget for 2028–2034, in a bid to bridge the gap between "frugals" and cohesion-minded member states. The figures, now on the table for the first time, will serve as the starting point for the sensitive negotiations ahead.

stories.headline: Zelensky under pressure to end row with Poland over WW2 name of army unit
stories.summary: Poland is threatening to strip Ukraine's president of a state honour over the naming Ukrainian military unit after controversial World War Two fighters.

stories.headline: US strikes Iran in response to downing of military helicopter
stories.summary: President Donald Trump earlier accused Iran of shooting down the US helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz and vowed to respond.

stories.headline: Four days of extreme rain in Indonesia killed 7% of world’s rarest great apes, study finds
stories.summary: Critically endangered Tapanuli orangutan population falls after heavy rain and landslides, fuelled by climate crisis, in North SumatraExtreme rainfall and landslides fuelled by the climate crisis killed 7% of the remaining population of the world’s rarest great ape, a study has found, prompting fears for the species’ survival.The research suggests 58 out of the remaining 800 critically endangered Tapanuli orangutans (Pongo tapanuliensis) were kil

stories.headline: Fear of the SaaSpocalypse is tormenting techland
stories.summary: Software once ate the world. Now it is in danger of eating itself

stories.headline: Bill Gates says Epstein wanted personal relationship, but he 'never reciprocated'
stories.summary: The Microsoft co-founder spent hours talking to lawmakers behind closed doors, where he also said Epstein used his marital infidelities to pressure him.

stories.headline: Pakistan launches deadly air strikes in Afghanistan, reigniting tensions
stories.summary: The fresh strikes come after weeks of relative calm in the restive border region between the two countries.

stories.headline: 'I will never get over watching my home burn': Belfast residents reel after night of unrest
stories.summary: A night of disorder in Belfast saw people forced to flee their homes, in protests that followed a knife attack in the north of the city.

stories.headline: Three Indian sailors missing after US says it hit tanker in Gulf of Oman
stories.summary: India's government said 21 crew had been rescued after the hit on the Settebello off the coast of Oman.

stories.headline: Nancy Mace’s thrashing in South Carolina governor’s race caps a rough downfall
stories.summary: The congresswoman finished fifth in the state’s GOP gubernatorial primary, according to unofficial returns, solidly losing even her own home county and district.

stories.summary: Amazon and Sony among firms that may have sourced coltan, used in phones, from supply chains controlled by the M23 rebels, says Global WitnessLeading global brands including Amazon, Ericsson and Sony are “likely” to have sourced minerals linked to a militia accused of widespread sexual violence, summary executions and torture, a new investigation claims.The companies allegedly, but unknowingly, acquired coltan smuggled from mines in the Democrati

stories.headline: Bowen: Trump and Netanyahu wanted to reshape the Middle East - now they risk a permacrisis
stories.summary: The US and Israeli leaders have lost control of the consequences after miscalculating the Iran war.

stories.headline: Iran and Israel say they will pause strikes but warn of retaliation if ceasefire breached again
stories.summary: Israel's PM says his country is holding fire "at the moment", after Iran's armed forces said they had stopped military action.

stories.headline: Inside Myanmar, rebels are losing ground as military forces men into army
stories.summary: The BBC travels with rebels to frontline positions in Myanmar to see how the war is unfolding.

stories.headline: Peru election result close as vote counting continues
stories.summary: The race between right-wing Keiko Fujimori and left-wing Roberto Sánchez has been dominated by concerns over crime and political instability.

stories.headline: Murder of Lyhanna, 11, enrages France and turns up heat on government
stories.summary: Protesters are angry that the suspect had already been reported to police last August in a separate case.

stories.headline: World's largest chipmaker does not rule out price rises as costs increase
stories.summary: In a rare interview, a senior executive at TSMC discusses the AI boom, the geopolitics of chips and what it means for the price of electronics.

stories.headline: Man shot dead during protest against proposed US Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya
stories.summary: Police dispersed demonstrators in Nanyuki, 120 miles from Nairobi, amid rising anger at US plansKenyan police have shot dead a man during a protest against a proposed Ebola quarantine facility for US citizens.Patrick Wahome, who has organised protests in Nanyuki against the centre, told Reuters on Tuesday the man died from a gunshot wound to the head. Reporters from the agency saw his body lying motionless in a police van with a large head wound.

stories.headline: Russia's fuel crisis intensifies as Ukraine steps up strikes on occupied territories
stories.summary: Kyiv has made it difficult for Moscow to provide military and civilian supplies to the territories it occupies.

stories.headline: Iran's World Cup team lands in Mexico amid US visa row
stories.summary: All of Iran's group games are in the US, but players and support staff will have to fly in and out of the country on match day.

stories.headline: 'No dead ends': What the Dutch can teach us about tackling youth unemployment
stories.summary: The Netherlands has one of the world's lowest rates of 16 to 24-year-olds not in education, employment or training.

stories.headline: Armenia votes as Russia piles pressure on pro-West government
stories.summary: Incumbent Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan is seeking a third term despite falling domestic support.

stories.headline: Funeral held for baby shot dead by Israeli troops in occupied West Bank
stories.summary: The seven-month-old boy's shrouded body was wrapped in a Palestinian flag and carried by his father to the graveyard.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange strikes in Gulf in latest test of ceasefire
stories.summary: The US military strikes Iranian drones and radar sites and Tehran says it has targeted US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain.
